methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series B → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.9x) to $140M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 4 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Robotics & Manufacturing. Range: $1.0B - $1.8B.

private.name: Bedrock Robotics
private.rationale: Same sector (Robotics & Manufacturing), same stage (Series B), post-money $1.75B

private.name: Dexterity
private.rationale: Same sector (Robotics & Manufacturing), same stage (Series B), post-money $1.65B

private.name: The Bot Company
private.rationale: Same sector (Robotics & Manufacturing), same stage (Series B)

data.narrative:
Sereact, a Series B robotics and manufacturing company founded by Ralf Gulde and Marc Tuscher, is evaluated against a comp universe of 9 private peers and 1 public comparable (277810.KQ). The private peer set includes Bedrock Robotics, Dexterity, Divergent, Exotec, and Locus Robotics, with stage concentration in Series B and C.

Two valuation methods were applied. Stage-progression analysis, using total raised of $140M as a proxy for current value, applies a 1.9x step-up multiplier typical of Series B advancement, yielding $266M. Peer-median valuation triangulates across four directly comparable companies: Bedrock Robotics at $1.8B (Series B), Dexterity at $1.6B (Series B), Hadrian at $1.6B (Series C), and Nimble Robotics at $1.0B (Series C). This produces a median implied valuation of $1.6B, with a range of $1.0B to $1.8B.

The triangulated analysis across both methods supports a valuation range of $226M to $1.9B, with a midpoint of $946M. The wide dispersion reflects material uncertainty: stage-progression relies on capital raised as a weak value proxy and assumes uniform step-up behavior, while peer-median valuations may not fully account for Sereact's specific unit economics, market position, or technology differentiation. The $1.6B peer median likely represents a more relevant anchor given comparable company stage and sector alignment, though confirmation would require visibility into Sereact's revenue, burn rate, and margin trajectory relative to peers.
